speaker: Woman
en: Good morning.

speaker: Woman
en: How may I help you?

speaker: Man
en: Good morning.

speaker: Man
en: We spoke on the phone last week, and I'm ringing to get more details about the job.

speaker: Woman
en: That's right.

speaker: Woman
en: Thank you for sending in your CV.

speaker: Woman
en: We've carefully looked at it, and I think we have several jobs that might suit you.

speaker: Man
en: That's great!

speaker: Woman
en: The first one is in a magazine based in North London.

speaker: Woman
en: They're looking for a reporter.

speaker: Man
en: What sort of magazine is it?

speaker: Woman
en: They're called Home Solutions, which publishes articles on housing every week, and they want to find someone responsible for collecting the latest news and writing relevant articles.

speaker: Man
en: What are the hours of work?

speaker: Woman
en: Well, you will be expected to work forty hours a week, but there may be opportunities to do overtime during busy periods.

speaker: Woman
en: For example, some may work more than fifty hours a week and get extra pay.

speaker: Woman
en: But you're not required to do this.

speaker: Man
en: That's reasonable.

speaker: Man
en: Do you know what the rates of pay are?

speaker: Woman
en: Yes.

speaker: Woman
en: It says you'll get a monthly pay of 7000 pounds for the first six months.

speaker: Woman
en: And after the probationary period, you'll be eligible for a salary of more than that, maybe 7500 pounds or more.

speaker: Man
en: That's good.

speaker: Woman
en: So, is that something that might interest you?

speaker: Man
en: It is, yes.

speaker: Man
en: The only thing is, you say they are in North London, so it would be a pretty long commute for me.

speaker: Woman
en: In that case, the second position might suit you better regarding location.

speaker: Woman
en: That's a researcher for an electronic engineering company in South London.

speaker: Man
en: Yes, that would be a lot closer.

speaker: Woman
en: Your CV shows you have relevant working experience in this field.

speaker: Man
en: Yes, I worked in the office of a computer engineering company for two years.

speaker: Man
en: What about the working hours?

speaker: Woman
en: For this job, you'll have to work 45 hours a week and the starting salary is 7200 pounds for the six-month probationary period.

speaker: Man
en: That should be nice.

speaker: Woman
en: I can arrange for you an interview if you're interested.

speaker: Woman
en: But before that, may I ask where you heard about us?

speaker: Woman
en: Was it in an advert or did your friend tell you?

speaker: Man
en: Neither.

speaker: Man
en: It was a website.

speaker: Man
en: Then I just got your number from the phone book.

speaker: Woman
en: Oh, right.

speaker: Woman
en: Apart from full-time jobs, we have some part-time work that might suit you.

speaker: Woman
en: Here several magazines are recruiting part-time writers.

speaker: Woman
en: Are you interested?

speaker: Man
en: What kind of writer exactly?

speaker: Man
en: I mean, what types of articles should I write about?

speaker: Woman
en: Well, the first one is to do with sports reports.

speaker: Woman
en: For this, you're expected to write sports articles twice weekly.

speaker: Man
en: Well, I love sports.

speaker: Man
en: I'm a football fan.

speaker: Man
en: But as far as writing is concerned, I'm not entirely keen on that.

speaker: Man
en: I think I'll rule it out.

speaker: Woman
en: OK.

speaker: Woman
en: Then how about writing something for entertainment?

speaker: Woman
en: Does it appeal to you?

speaker: Man
en: Entertainment is an exciting topic.

speaker: Man
en: I like reading news or articles in this category, but I'm not sure if I should write something about it.

speaker: Man
en: Anyway, I'll think about that.

speaker: Man
en: Could you give me more details?

speaker: Woman
en: OK, I've got your email, and I will send the details to you later.

speaker: Man
en: Thanks.

speaker: Woman
en: And the third one is writing reviews for soap dramas.

speaker: Man
en: Oh, soap drama is my favourite.

speaker: Man
en: I worked as a freelance writer in this field three years ago.

speaker: Man
en: I think it's pretty worthwhile to give my own opinions and recommendations to the audience.

speaker: Woman
en: That's good.

speaker: Woman
en: And the last one is writing articles for a business economy magazine.

speaker: Man
en: I'm unfamiliar with this field, so I think it might not be easy.

speaker: Woman
en: Don't worry too much.

speaker: Woman
en: Experience is not essential for this job.

speaker: Woman
en: They've got a training session for every recruit.

speaker: Man
en: That sounds not too bad.

speaker: Woman
en: OK, would you like me to send you the pamphlet so that you can learn more about it?

speaker: Man
en: That will be nice.

speaker: Woman
en: OK, that's it.

speaker: Woman
en: I'll send you all the information later and ...
